verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem princeps sacerdotum: Si hæc ita se habent?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ ἀρχιερεύς εἰ ἄρα ταῦτα οὕτως ἔχει
verses.king_james: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait: Viri fratres et patres, audite: Deus gloriæ apparuit patri nostro Abrahæ cum esset in Mesopotamia, priusquam moraretur in Charan,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἔφη ἄνδρες ἀδελφοὶ καὶ πατέρες ἀκούσατε ὁ θεὸς τῆς δόξης ὤφθη τῷ πατρὶ ἡμῶν ἀβραὰμ ὄντι ἐν τῇ μεσοποταμίᾳ πρὶν ἢ κατοικῆσαι αὐτὸν ἐν χαῤῥάν
verses.king_james: And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit ad illum: Exi de terra tua, et de cognatione tua, et veni in terram quam monstravero tibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν ἔξελθε ἐκ τῆς γῆς σου καὶ ἐκ τῆς συγγενείας σου καὶ δεῦρο εἰς γῆν ἣν ἄν σοι δείξω
verses.king_james: And said unto him, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc exiit de terra Chaldæorum, et habitavit in Charan. Et inde, postquam mortuus est pater ejus, transtulit illum in terram istam, in qua nunc vos habitatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐξελθὼν ἐκ γῆς χαλδαίων κατῴκησεν ἐν χαῤῥάν κἀκεῖθεν μετὰ τὸ ἀποθανεῖν τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ μετῴκισεν αὐτὸν εἰς τὴν γῆν ταύτην εἰς ἣν ὑμεῖς νῦν κατοικεῖτε
verses.king_james: Then came he out of the land of the Chaldaeans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non dedit illi hæreditatem in ea, nec passum pedis: sed repromisit dare illi eam in possessionem, et semini ejus post ipsum, cum non haberet filium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ κληρονομίαν ἐν αὐτῇ οὐδὲ βῆμα ποδός καὶ ἐπηγγείλατο αὐτῷ δοῦναι εἰς κατάσχεσιν αὐτὴν καὶ τῷ σπέρματι αὐτοῦ μετ᾽ αὐτόν οὐκ ὄντος αὐτῷ τέκνου
verses.king_james: And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not *so much as* to set his foot on: yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when *as yet* he had no child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locutus est autem ei Deus: Quia erit semen ejus accola in terra aliena, et servituti eos subjicient, et male tractabunt eos annis quadringentis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλάλησεν δὲ οὕτως ὁ θεὸς ὅτι ἔσται τὸ σπέρμα αὐτοῦ πάροικον ἐν γῇ ἀλλοτρίᾳ καὶ δουλώσουσιν αὐτὸ καὶ κακώσουσιν ἔτη τετρακόσια
verses.king_james: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat *them* evil four hundred years.

verses.latin_vulgate: et gentem cui servierint, judicabo ego, dixit Dominus: et post hæc exibunt, et servient mihi in loco isto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὸ ἔθνος ᾧ ἐὰν δουλεύσωσιν κρινῶ ἐγώ εἶπεν ὁ θεὸς καὶ μετὰ ταῦτα ἐξελεύσονται καὶ λατρεύσουσίν μοι ἐν τῷ τόπῳ τούτῳ
verses.king_james: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dedit illi testamentum circumcisionis: et sic genuit Isaac, et circumcidit eum die octavo: et Isaac, Jacob: et Jacob, duodecim patriarchas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ διαθήκην περιτομῆς καὶ οὕτως ἐγέννησεν τὸν ἰσαὰκ καὶ περιέτεμεν αὐτὸν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ ὀγδόῃ καὶ ὁ ἰσαὰκ τὸν ἰακώβ καὶ ὁ ἰακὼβ τοὺς δώδεκα πατριάρχας
verses.king_james: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so *Abraham* begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac *begat* Jacob; and Jacob *begat* the twelve patriarchs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et patriarchæ æmulantes, Joseph vendiderunt in Ægyptum: et erat Deus cum eo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ πατριάρχαι ζηλώσαντες τὸν ἰωσὴφ ἀπέδοντο εἰς αἴγυπτον καὶ ἦν ὁ θεὸς μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,

verses.latin_vulgate: et eripuit eum ex omnibus tribulationibus ejus, et dedit ei gratiam et sapientiam in conspectu pharaonis regis Ægypti: et constituit eum præpositum super Ægyptum, et super omnem domum suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξείλετο αὐτὸν ἐκ πασῶν τῶν θλίψεων αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ χάριν καὶ σοφίαν ἐναντίον φαραὼ βασιλέως αἰγύπτου καὶ κατέστησεν αὐτὸν ἡγούμενον ἐπ᾽ αἴγυπτον καὶ ὅλον τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem fames in universam Ægyptum et Chanaan, et tribulatio magna: et non inveniebant cibos patres nostri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθεν δὲ λιμὸς ἐφ᾽ ὅλην τὴν γὴν αἰγύπτου καὶ χανάαν καὶ θλῖψις μεγάλη καὶ οὐχ εὕρισκον χορτάσματα οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum audisset autem Jacob esse frumentum in Ægypto, misit patres nostros primum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ἰακὼβ ὄντα σῖτά ἕν αἰγύπτῳ ἐξαπέστειλεν τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν πρῶτον
verses.king_james: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.

verses.latin_vulgate: et in secundo cognitus est Joseph a fratribus suis, et manifestatum est Pharaoni genus ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τῷ δευτέρῳ ἀνεγνωρίσθη ἰωσὴφ τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ φανερὸν ἐγένετο τῷ φαραὼ τὸ γένος τοῦ ἰωσήφ
verses.king_james: And at the second *time* Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph's kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mittens autem Joseph, accersivit Jacob patrem suum et omnem cognationem suam, in animabus septuaginta quinque.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποστείλας δὲ ἰωσὴφ μετεκαλέσατο τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ ἰακὼβ καὶ πᾶσαν τὴν συγγένειαν αὐτοῦ ἐν ψυχαῖς ἑβδομήκοντα πέντε
verses.king_james: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to *him*, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendit Jacob in Ægyptum: et defunctus est ipse, et patres nostri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατέβη δὲ ἰακὼβ εἰς αἴγυπτον καὶ ἐτελεύτησεν αὐτὸς καὶ οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et translati sunt in Sichem, et positi sunt in sepulchro, quod emit Abraham pretio argenti a filiis Hemor filii Sichem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μετετέθησαν εἰς συχὲμ καὶ ἐτέθησαν ἐν τῷ μνήματι ὅ ὠνήσατο ἀβραὰμ τιμῆς ἀργυρίου παρὰ τῶν υἱῶν ἑμμὸρ τοῦ συχέμ
verses.king_james: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor *the father* of Sychem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem appropinquaret tempus promissionis quam confessus erat Deus Abrahæ, crevit populus, et multiplicatus est in Ægypto,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς δὲ ἤγγιζεν ὁ χρόνος τῆς ἐπαγγελίας ἡς ὡμοσεν ὁ θεὸς τῷ ἀβραάμ ηὔξησεν ὁ λαὸς καὶ ἐπληθύνθη ἐν αἰγύπτῳ
verses.king_james: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,

verses.latin_vulgate: quoadusque surrexit alius rex in Ægypto, qui non sciebat Joseph.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄχρις οὗ ἀνέστη βασιλεὺς ἕτερος ὃς οὐκ ᾔδει τὸν ἰωσήφ
verses.king_james: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic circumveniens genus nostrum, afflixit patres nostros ut exponerent infantes suos, ne vivificarentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος κατασοφισάμενος τὸ γένος ἡμῶν ἐκάκωσεν τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν τοῦ ποιεῖν ἔκθετα τὰ βρέφη αὐτῶν εἰς τὸ μὴ ζῳογονεῖσθαι
verses.king_james: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eodem tempore natus est Moyses, et fuit gratus Deo: qui nutritus est tribus mensibus in domo patris sui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ᾧ καιρῷ ἐγεννήθη μωσῆς, καὶ ἦν ἀστεῖος τῷ θεῷ ὃς ἀνετράφη μῆνας τρεῖς ἐν τῷ οἴκῳ τοῦ πατρός αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father's house three months:

verses.latin_vulgate: Exposito autem illo, sustulit eum filia Pharaonis, et nutrivit eum sibi in filium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκτεθέντα δὲ αὐτὸν ἀνείλετο αὐτὸν ἡ θυγάτηρ φαραὼ καὶ ἀνεθρέψατο αὐτόν ἑαυτῇ εἰς υἱόν
verses.king_james: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh's daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et eruditus est Moyses omni sapientia Ægyptiorum, et erat potens in verbis et in operibus suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπαιδεύθη μωσῆς πάσῃ σοφίᾳ αἰγυπτίων ἦν δὲ δυνατὸς ἐν λόγοις καὶ ἐν ἔργοις
verses.king_james: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem impleretur ei quadraginta annorum tempus, ascendit in cor ejus ut visitaret fratres suos filios Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἐπληροῦτο αὐτῷ τεσσαρακονταετὴς χρόνος ἀνέβη ἐπὶ τὴν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ ἐπισκέψασθαι τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς αὐτοῦ τοὺς υἱοὺς ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum vidisset quemdam injuriam patientem, vindicavit illum, et fecit ultionem ei qui injuriam sustinebat, percusso Ægyptio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδών τινα ἀδικούμενον ἠμύνατο καὶ ἐποίησεν ἐκδίκησιν τῷ καταπονουμένῳ πατάξας τὸν αἰγύπτιον
verses.king_james: And seeing one *of them* suffer wrong, he defended *him*, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimabat autem intelligere fratres, quoniam Deus per manum ipsius daret salutem illis: at illi non intellexerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐνόμιζεν δὲ συνιέναι τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς αὐτοῦ ὅτι ὁ θεὸς διὰ χειρὸς αὐτοῦ δίδωσιν αὐτοῖς σωτηρίαν οἱ δὲ οὐ συνῆκαν
verses.king_james: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sequenti vero die apparuit illis litigantibus: et reconciliabat eos in pace, dicens: Viri, fratres estis: ut quid nocetis alterutrum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ τε ἐπιούσῃ ἡμέρᾳ ὤφθη αὐτοῖς μαχομένοις καὶ συνήλασεν αὐτοὺς εἰς εἰρήνην εἰπών ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί ἐστε ὑμεῖς ἱνατί ἀδικεῖτε ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem injuriam faciebat proximo, repulit eum, dicens: Quis te constituit principem et judicem super nos?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀδικῶν τὸν πλησίον ἀπώσατο αὐτὸν εἰπών τίς σε κατέστησεν ἄρχοντα καὶ δικαστὴν ἐφ᾽ ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid interficere me tu vis, quemadmodum interfecisti heri Ægyptium?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἀνελεῖν με σὺ θέλεις ὃν τρόπον ἀνεῖλες χθὲς τὸν αἰγύπτιον
verses.king_james: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugit autem Moyses in verbo isto: et factus est advena in terra Madian, ubi generavit filios duos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφυγεν δὲ μωσῆς ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τούτῳ καὶ ἐγένετο πάροικος ἐν γῇ μαδιάμ οὗ ἐγέννησεν υἱοὺς δύο
verses.king_james: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et expletis annis quadraginta, apparuit illi in deserto montis Sina angelus in igne flammæ rubi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πληρωθέντων ἐτῶν τεσσαράκοντα ὤφθη αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ τοῦ ὄρους σινᾶ ἄγγελος κυρίου ἐν φλογὶ πυρὸς βάτου
verses.king_james: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.

verses.latin_vulgate: Moyses autem videns, admiratus est visum. Et accedente illo ut consideraret, facta est ad eum vox Domini, dicens:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ μωσῆς ἰδὼν ἐθαύμασεν τὸ ὅραμα προσερχομένου δὲ αὐτοῦ κατανοῆσαι ἐγένετο φωνὴ κυρίου πρὸς αὐτὸν,
verses.king_james: When Moses saw *it*, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold *it*, the voice of the Lord came unto him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum Deus patrum tuorum, Deus Abraham, Deus Isaac, et Deus Jacob. Tremefactus autem Moyses, non audebat considerare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων σου ὁ θεὸς ἀβραὰμ καὶ ὁ θεὸς ἰσαὰκ καὶ ὁ θεὸς ἰακώβ ἔντρομος δὲ γενόμενος μωσῆς οὐκ ἐτόλμα κατανοῆσαι
verses.king_james: *Saying*, I *am* the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem illi Dominus: Solve calceamentum pedum tuorum: locus enim in quo stas, terra sancta est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος λῦσον τὸ ὑπόδημα τῶν ποδῶν σου ὁ γὰρ τόπος ἐν ᾧ ἕστηκας γῆ ἁγία ἐστίν
verses.king_james: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns vidi afflictionem populi mei qui est in Ægypto, et gemitum eorum audivi, et descendi liberare eos. Et nunc veni, et mittam te in Ægyptum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν εἶδον τὴν κάκωσιν τοῦ λαοῦ μου τοῦ ἐν αἰγύπτῳ καὶ τοῦ στεναγμοῦ αὐτῶν ἤκουσα καὶ κατέβην ἐξελέσθαι αὐτούς καὶ νῦν δεῦρο ἀποστέλω σε εἰς αἴγυπτον
verses.king_james: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and i have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hunc Moysen, quem negaverunt, dicentes: Quis te constituit principem et judicem? hunc Deus principem et redemptorem misit, cum manu angeli qui apparuit illi in rubo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτον τὸν μωϋσῆν ὃν ἠρνήσαντο εἰπόντες τίς σε κατέστησεν ἄρχοντα καὶ δικαστήν τοῦτον ὁ θεὸς ἄρχοντα καὶ λυτρωτὴν ἀπέστειλεν ἐν χειρὶ ἀγγέλου τοῦ ὀφθέντος αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ βάτῳ
verses.king_james: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send *to be* a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic eduxit illos faciens prodigia et signa in terra Ægypti, et in rubro mari, et in deserto annis quadraginta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἐξήγαγεν αὐτοὺς ποιήσας τέρατα καὶ σημεῖα ἐν γῇ αἰγύπτοῦ καὶ ἐν ἐρυθρᾷ θαλάσσῃ καὶ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἔτη τεσσαράκοντα
verses.king_james: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic est Moyses, qui dixit filiis Israël: Prophetam suscitabit vobis Deus de fratribus vestris, tamquam me: ipsum audietis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ μωϋσῆς ὁ εἰπὼν τοῖς υἱοῖς ἰσραήλ προφήτην ὑμῖν ἀναστήσει κύριος ὁ θεὸς ὑμῶν ἐκ τῶν ἀδελφῶν ὑμῶν ὡς ἐμέ αὐτοῦ ἀκούσεσθε
verses.king_james: This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic est qui fuit in ecclesia in solitudine cum angelo, qui loquebatur ei in monte Sina, et cum patribus nostris: qui accepit verba vitæ dare nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ γενόμενος ἐν τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ μετὰ τοῦ ἀγγέλου τοῦ λαλοῦντος αὐτῷ ἐν τῷ ὄρει σινᾶ καὶ τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν ὃς ἐδέξατο λόγια ζῶντα δοῦναι ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and *with* our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui noluerunt obedire patres nostri: sed repulerunt, et aversi sunt cordibus suis in Ægyptum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ οὐκ ἠθέλησαν ὑπήκοοι γενέσθαι οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν ἀλλ᾽ ἀπώσαντο καὶ ἐστράφησαν ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν εἰς αἴγυπτον
verses.king_james: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust *him* from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes ad Aaron: Fac nobis deos qui præcedant nos: Moyses enim hic, qui eduxit nos de terra Ægypti, nescimus quid factum sit ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰπόντες τῷ ἀαρών ποίησον ἡμῖν θεοὺς οἳ προπορεύσονται ἡμῶν ὁ γὰρ μωσῆς οὗτος ὃς ἐξήγαγεν ἡμᾶς ἐκ γῆς αἰγύπτου οὐκ οἴδαμεν τί γέγονεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for *as for* this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vitulum fecerunt in diebus illis, et obtulerunt hostiam simulacro, et lætabantur in operibus manuum suarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμοσχοποίησαν ἐν ταῖς ἡμέραις ἐκείναις καὶ ἀνήγαγον θυσίαν τῷ εἰδώλῳ καὶ εὐφραίνοντο ἐν τοῖς ἔργοις τῶν χειρῶν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convertit autem Deus, et tradidit eos servire militiæ cæli, sicut scriptum est in libro prophetarum: [Numquid victimas et hostias obtulistis mihi annis quadraginta in deserto, domus Israël?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔστρεψεν δὲ ὁ θεὸς καὶ παρέδωκεν αὐτοὺς λατρεύειν τῇ στρατιᾷ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καθὼς γέγραπται ἐν βίβλῳ τῶν προφητῶν μὴ σφάγια καὶ θυσίας προσηνέγκατέ μοι ἔτη τεσσαράκοντα ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ οἶκος ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices *by the space of* forty years in the wilderness?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et suscepistis tabernaculum Moloch, et sidus dei vestri Rempham, figuras quas fecistis adorare eas: et transferam vos trans Babylonem.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀνελάβετε τὴν σκηνὴν τοῦ μολὸχ καὶ τὸ ἄστρον τοῦ θεοῦ ὑμῶν ῥεμφὰν, τοὺς τύπους οὓς ἐποιήσατε προσκυνεῖν αὐτοῖς καὶ μετοικιῶ ὑμᾶς ἐπέκεινα βαβυλῶνος
verses.king_james: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabernaculum testimonii fuit cum patribus nostris in deserto, sicut disposuit illis Deus loquens ad Moysen, ut faceret illud secundum formam quam viderat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ἦν ἐν τοῖς πατράσιν ἡμῶν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ καθὼς διετάξατο ὁ λαλῶν τῷ μωσῇ, ποιῆσαι αὐτὴν κατὰ τὸν τύπον ὃν ἑωράκει
verses.king_james: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod et induxerunt, suscipientes patres nostri cum Jesu in possessionem gentium quas expulit Deus a facie patrum nostrorum, usque in diebus David,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἣν καὶ εἰσήγαγον διαδεξάμενοι οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν μετὰ ἰησοῦ ἐν τῇ κατασχέσει τῶν ἐθνῶν ὧν ἐξῶσεν ὁ θεὸς ἀπὸ προσώπου τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν ἕως τῶν ἡμερῶν δαβίδ
verses.king_james: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui invenit gratiam ante Deum, et petiit ut inveniret tabernaculum Deo Jacob.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς εὗρεν χάριν ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ᾐτήσατο εὑρεῖν σκήνωμα τῷ θεῷ ἰακώβ
verses.king_james: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salomon autem ædificavit illi domum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σολομῶν δὲ ὠκοδόμησεν αὐτῷ οἶκον
verses.king_james: But Solomon built him an house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed non Excelsus in manufactis habitat, sicut propheta dicit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ οὐχ ὁ ὕψιστος ἐν χειροποιήτοις ναοῖς κατοικεῖ καθὼς ὁ προφήτης λέγει
verses.king_james: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,

verses.latin_vulgate: [Cælum mihi sedes est: terra autem scabellum pedum meorum. Quam domum ædificabitis mihi? dicit Dominus: aut quis locus requietionis meæ est?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὐρανός μοι θρόνος ἡ δὲ γῆ ὑποπόδιον τῶν ποδῶν μου ποῖον οἶκον οἰκοδομήσετέ μοι λέγει κύριος ἢ τίς τόπος τῆς καταπαύσεώς μου
verses.king_james: Heaven *is* my throne, and earth *is* my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what *is* the place of my rest?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne manus mea fecit hæc omnia?]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχὶ ἡ χείρ μου ἐποίησεν ταῦτα πάντα
verses.king_james: Hath not my hand made all these things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dura cervice, et incircumcisis cordibus et auribus, vos semper Spiritui Sancto resistitis: sicut patres vestri, ita et vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σκληροτράχηλοι καὶ ἀπερίτμητοι τῇ καρδίᾳ καὶ τοῖς ὠσίν ὑμεῖς ἀεὶ τῷ πνεύματι τῷ ἁγίῳ ἀντιπίπτετε ὡς οἱ πατέρες ὑμῶν καὶ ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers *did*, so *do* ye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem prophetarum non sunt persecuti patres vestri? et occiderunt eos qui prænuntiabant de adventu Justi, cujus vos nunc proditores et homicidæ fuistis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίνα τῶν προφητῶν οὐκ ἐδίωξαν οἱ πατέρες ὑμῶν καὶ ἀπέκτειναν τοὺς προκαταγγείλαντας περὶ τῆς ἐλεύσεως τοῦ δικαίου οὗ νῦν ὑμεῖς προδόται καὶ φονεῖς γεγένησθε
verses.king_james: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui accepistis legem in dispositione angelorum, et non custodistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες ἐλάβετε τὸν νόμον εἰς διαταγὰς ἀγγέλων καὶ οὐκ ἐφυλάξατε
verses.king_james: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audientes autem hæc, dissecabantur cordibus suis, et stridebant dentibus in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούοντες δὲ ταῦτα διεπρίοντο ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν καὶ ἔβρυχον τοὺς ὀδόντας ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with *their* teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem esset plenus Spiritu Sancto, intendens in cælum, vidit gloriam Dei, et Jesum stantem a dextris Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπάρχων δὲ πλήρης πνεύματος ἁγίου ἀτενίσας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν εἶδεν δόξαν θεοῦ καὶ ἰησοῦν ἑστῶτα ἐκ δεξιῶν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait: Ecce video cælos apertos, et Filium hominis stantem a dextris Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ἰδού, θεωρῶ τοὺς οὐρανοὺς ἀνεῳγμένους καὶ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκ δεξιῶν ἑστῶτα τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exclamantes autem voce magna continuerunt aures suas, et impetum fecerunt unanimiter in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κράξαντες δὲ φωνῇ μεγάλῃ συνέσχον τὰ ὦτα αὐτῶν καὶ ὥρμησαν ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ejicientes eum extra civitatem, lapidabant: et testes deposuerunt vestimenta sua secus pedes adolescentis qui vocabatur Saulus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκβαλόντες ἔξω τῆς πόλεως ἐλιθοβόλουν καὶ οἱ μάρτυρες ἀπέθεντο τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτῶν παρὰ τοὺς πόδας νεανίου καλουμένου σαύλου
verses.king_james: And cast *him* out of the city, and stoned *him*: and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man's feet, whose name was Saul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et lapidabant Stephanum invocantem, et dicentem: Domine Jesu, suscipe spiritum meum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλιθοβόλουν τὸν στέφανον ἐπικαλούμενον καὶ λέγοντα κύριε ἰησοῦ δέξαι τὸ πνεῦμά μου
verses.king_james: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon *God*, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Positis autem genibus, clamavit voce magna, dicens: Domine, ne statuas illis hoc peccatum. Et cum hoc dixisset, obdormivit in Domino. Saulus autem erat consentiens neci ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεὶς δὲ τὰ γόνατα ἔκραξεν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ κύριε μὴ στήσῃς αὐτοῖς τὴν ἁμαρτίαν ταύτην καὶ τοῦτο εἰπὼν ἐκοιμήθη
verses.king_james: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
